days.title: Arrival in Hong Kong
days.subtitle: Exploring Tsim Sha Tsui

timeline.title: Arrive at Hong Kong International Airport
timeline.detail: After your flight, head to the city by Airport Express. It's a quick and convenient ride to Central.

timeline.title: Check in to Hotel ICON
timeline.detail: Settle into your stylish room at this luxury hotel within a 10-minute walk from Tsim Sha Tsui. Enjoy stunning views of Victoria Harbour.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit Victoria Harbour
timeline.detail: Enjoy a leisurely stroll along the waterfront promenade at Tsim Sha Tsui with picturesque views of the iconic Hong Kong skyline, and snap some amazing photos.
timeline.mapQuery: Victoria Harbour, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Dinner at Hutong
timeline.detail: Savor northern Chinese cuisine with a view at this upscale restaurant, known for its dumplings and stunning skyline views. Reservations recommended.
timeline.mapQuery: Hutong, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: A Symphony of Lights Show
timeline.detail: Watch the spectacular light and sound show at Victoria Harbour, featuring iconic buildings illuminated in vibrant colors — best viewed from the promenade.
timeline.mapQuery: Tsim Sha Tsui Promenade, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore the Night Market
timeline.detail: Take a stroll through the Temple Street Night Market. Enjoy local street food and shop for souvenirs while experiencing the vibrant local atmosphere.
timeline.mapQuery: Temple Street Night Market, Kowloon, Hong Kong

localTips: Purchase an Octopus card at the airport for easy public transport access.
Dining reservations at popular spots like Hutong are highly recommended.
Street food is a must-try; don't skip the egg waffles and stinky tofu.
Check ferry timings if you want to explore Hong Kong Island.
Bring comfortable shoes for walking around Tsim Sha Tsui.

days.title: Discovering Hong Kong's Culture
days.subtitle: From museums to local delicacies

timeline.title: Breakfast at Australian Dairy Company
timeline.detail: Start your day with a traditional Hong Kong breakfast of toast, eggs, and milk tea at this popular diner. Arrive early to beat the crowds.
timeline.mapQuery: Australian Dairy Company, Yau Ma Tei, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Hong Kong Museum of History
timeline.detail: Explore the rich history of Hong Kong through engaging exhibits that cover its cultural and historical evolution. A must-visit for history buffs.
timeline.mapQuery: Hong Kong Museum of History, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Lunch at Mak's Noodle
timeline.detail: Taste one of Hong Kong’s famous wonton noodle soups at this beloved eatery. Be ready for a small yet delicious bowl of authentic flavors.

timeline.title: Visit Kowloon Walled City Park
timeline.detail: Explore this historic park built on the site of a former military stronghold, showcasing gardens, ponds, and remnants of the old city walls.
timeline.mapQuery: Kowloon Walled City Park, Kowloon, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Tea Time at The Peninsula Hong Kong
timeline.detail: Indulge in a luxurious afternoon tea experience at The Peninsula, featuring exquisite pastries and a selection of fine teas for an elegant break.
timeline.mapQuery: The Peninsula Hong Kong, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Explore Mong Kok
timeline.detail: Spend your evening in the buzzing streets of Mong Kok. Shop at the Ladies' Market or search for hidden gems in its electronic market.

localTips: Try the HK-style milk tea at local cafes — it's a local favorite.
Mong Kok can get very crowded; visit in the evening for the best atmosphere.
Consider a guided bike tour to cover more historical sites efficiently.
Check if any special exhibitions are at the Museum of History during your visit.
Be mindful of opening hours, especially for museums and shops.

days.title: Nature and Relaxation
days.subtitle: Scenic Views and Leisurely Moments

timeline.title: Breakfast at the Hotel
timeline.detail: Enjoy a relaxed breakfast at the hotel restaurant, featuring a delightful mix of local and international dishes to start your day.
timeline.mapQuery: Hotel ICON Restaurant, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Visit The Peak
timeline.detail: Take the Peak Tram to Victoria Peak, where you can enjoy breathtaking views of the city. Don’t forget your camera!

timeline.title: Lunch at Cafe Deco
timeline.detail: Relax at this popular café located at The Peak with outdoor seating and panoramic views. Choose from a diverse menu of Western dishes.
timeline.mapQuery: Cafe Deco, Victoria Peak, Hong Kong

timeline.title: Hiking at Hong Kong Trail
timeline.detail: Embark on a scenic hike along the Hong Kong Trail, taking in lush greenery and stunning views of the island. Perfect for nature lovers.

timeline.title: Return to Tsim Sha Tsui
timeline.detail: Make your way back to Tsim Sha Tsui and take the opportunity to relax at a café or explore some last-minute shopping.

timeline.title: Dinner at Jumbo Kingdom
timeline.detail: Enjoy a unique dining experience on this floating restaurant. Savor Cantonese cuisine while enjoying the harbor view.

localTips: The Peak Tram is popular; arrive early or book a timed ticket.
Wear sturdy shoes for hiking; some trails can be steep.
Take the bus back from The Peak if you want a scenic route.
Look for the iconic red taxi for easy transport around the city.
Keep your camera ready; there are many photo opportunities along the trails.

hotels.name: Hotel ICON
hotels.note: Luxury hotel near Tsim Sha Tsui, excellent views.
hotels.address: 17 Science Museum Road, Tsim Sha Tsui, Hong Kong

budget.note: Estimated costs for history museums and attractions.

urgent.label: Book afternoon tea at The Peninsula
urgent.note: Highly recommended to book in advance due to popularity.

urgent.label: Reserve table at Hutong
urgent.note: Reservations are strongly recommended for dinner.

urgent.label: Purchase Octopus Card
urgent.note: Preload your Octopus card for convenience on public transport.
